    98→class TokenType(Enum):
    99→    IDENTIFIER = "IDENTIFIER"
   100→    NUMBER = "NUMBER"
   101→    LPAREN = "("
   102→    RPAREN = ")"
   103→    COMMA = ","
   104→    EQUALS = "="
   105→    NEWLINE = "NEWLINE"
   106→    EOF = "EOF"
   107→
   108→
   109→@dataclass
   110→class Token:
   111→    type: TokenType
   112→    value: str
   113→    line: int
   114→    col: int
   115→
   116→
   117→class Tokenizer:
   118→    """Tokenizer for .circ files."""
   119→
   120→    def __init__(self, text: str, filename: str):
   121→        self.text = text
   122→        self.filename = filename
   123→        self.pos = 0
   124→        self.line = 1
   125→        self.col = 1
   126→        self.tokens: list[Token] = []
   127→
   128→    def tokenize(self) -> list[Token]:
   129→        while self.pos < len(self.text):
   130→            ch = self.text[self.pos]
   131→            start_pos = self.pos
   132→
   133→            if ch.isspace():
   134→                if ch == '\n':
   135→                    self._add_token(TokenType.NEWLINE, '\n', self.line, self.col)
   136→                    self.line += 1
   137→                    self.col = 1
   138→                    self.pos += 1
   139→                else:
   140→                    self.col += 1
   141→                    self.pos += 1
   142→            elif ch == '#':
   143→                # Skip comment until end of line
   144→                while self.pos < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos] != '\n':
   145→                    self.pos += 1
   146→                    self.col += 1
   147→            elif ch.isalpha() or ch == '_':
   148→                start_line = self.line
   149→                start_col = self.col
   150→                while self.pos < len(self.text) and (self.text[self.pos].isalnum() or self.text[self.pos] == '_'):
   151→                    self.pos += 1
   152→                    self.col += 1
   153→                value = self.text[start_col - 1:self.pos]
   154→                self._add_token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, value, start_line, start_col)
   155→            elif ch.isdigit():
   156→                start_line = self.line
   157→                start_col = self.col
   158→                while self.pos < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos].isdigit():
   159→                    self.pos += 1
   160→                    self.col += 1
   161→                value = self.text[start_col - 1:self.pos]
   162→                self._add_token(TokenType.NUMBER, value, start_line, start_col)
   163→            elif ch == '(':
   164→                self._add_token(TokenType.LPAREN, '(', self.line, self.col)
   165→                self.pos += 1
   166→                self.col += 1
   167→            elif ch == ')':
   168→                self._add_token(TokenType.RPAREN, ')', self.line, self.col)
   169→                self.pos += 1
   170→                self.col += 1
   171→            elif ch == ',':
   172→                self._add_token(TokenType.COMMA, ',', self.line, self.col)
   173→                self.pos += 1
   174→                self.col += 1
   175→            elif ch == '=':
   176→                self._add_token(TokenType.EQUALS, '=', self.line, self.col)
   177→                self.pos += 1
   178→                self.col += 1
   179→            else:
   180→                raise CircParseError(
   181→                    f"Unexpected character '{ch}'",
   182→                    self.filename, self.line, self.col
   183→                )
   184→
   185→        self._add_token(TokenType.EOF, '', self.line, self.col)
   186→        return self.tokens
   187→
   188→    def _add_token(self, token_type: TokenType, value: str, line: int = None, col: int = None):
   189→        if line is None:
   190→            line = self.line
   191→        if col is None:
   192→            col = self.col
   193→        self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, line, col))

   196→class Parser:
   197→    """Parser for .circ files."""
   198→
   199→    # Supported operators and their arities
   200→    OPERATORS = {
   201→        'NOT': 1,
   202→        'BUF': 1,
   203→        'AND': -1,  # >= 2
   204→        'OR': -1,
   205→        'XOR': -1,
   206→        'NAND': -1,
   207→        'NOR': -1,
   208→        'XNOR': -1,
   209→    }
   210→
   211→    def __init__(self, tokens: list[Token], filename: str):
   212→        self.tokens = tokens
   213→        self.filename = filename
   214→        self.pos = 0
   215→        self.assignments_started = False
   216→
   217→    def parse(self) -> dict:
   218→        """Parse the circuit file and return declarations and assignments."""
   219→        result = {
   220→            'inputs': set(),
   221→            'outputs': set(),
   222→            'wires': set(),
   223→            'assignments': [],  # list of (lhs, rhs)
   224→        }
   225→
   226→        # First pass: parse declarations
   227→        while self.pos < len(self.tokens):
   228→            token = self.peek()
   229→            if token.type == TokenType.EOF:
   230→                break
   231→            elif token.type == TokenType.NEWLINE:
   232→                self.consume()
   233→                continue
   234→
   235→            # Check if this is a declaration
   236→            if token.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and token.value.upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE'):
   237→                decl_type = token.value.upper()
   238→                if self.assignments_started:
   239→                    raise CircParseError(
   240→                        f"Declaration after assignment: {decl_type}",
   241→                        self.filename, token.line, token.col
   242→                    )
   243→                self._parse_declaration(decl_type, result)
   244→            elif token.type == TokenType.NEWLINE:
   245→                self.consume()
   246→            else:
   247→                # Assignment - this starts the assignment phase
   248→                self.assignments_started = True
   249→                self._parse_assignment(result)
   250→
   251→        # Validate that all outputs and wires are assigned
   252→        assigned_names = set()
   253→        for lhs, _ in result['assignments']:
   254→            assigned_names.add(lhs)
   255→
   256→        all_signals = result['inputs'] | result['outputs'] | result['wires']
   257→
   258→        # Check for unassigned outputs and wires
   259→        for name in result['outputs'] | result['wires']:
   260→            if name not in assigned_names:
   261→                raise UnassignedSignalError(f"Signal '{name}' is not assigned")
   262→
   263→        # Check for duplicate assignments
   264→        seen_assignments = set()
   265→        for lhs, _ in result['assignments']:
   266→            if lhs in seen_assignments:
   267→                raise MultipleAssignmentError(f"Signal '{lhs}' is assigned multiple times")
   268→            seen_assignments.add(lhs)
   269→
   270→        return result
   271→
   272→    def _parse_declaration(self, decl_type: str, result: dict):
   273→        """Parse a declaration line."""
   274→        # Consume the keyword
   275→        self.consume()
   276→
   277→        # Parse names
   278→        names = []
   279→        while self.pos < len(self.tokens):
   280→            token = self.peek()
   281→            if token.type in (TokenType.NEWLINE, TokenType.EOF):
   282→                break
   283→            if token.type != TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   284→                raise CircParseError(
   285→                    f"Expected identifier, got '{token.value}'",
   286→                    self.filename, token.line, token.col
   287→                )
   288→            # Check for duplicate
   289→            name = token.value
   290→            target_set = {'INPUT': result['inputs'], 'OUTPUT': result['outputs'], 'WIRE': result['wires']}[decl_type]
   291→
   292→            # Check for duplicate across all signals
   293→            all_signals = result['inputs'] | result['outputs'] | result['wires']
   294→            if name in all_signals:
   295→                raise DuplicateNameError(f"Duplicate name '{name}'")
   296→
   297→            target_set.add(name)
   298→            names.append(name)
   299→            self.consume()
   300→
   301→        # Consume newline
   302→        if self.pos < len(self.tokens) and self.peek().type == TokenType.NEWLINE:
   303→            self.consume()
   304→
   305→        return names
   306→
   307→    def _parse_assignment(self, result: dict):
   308→        """Parse an assignment line."""
   309→        # Parse LHS
   310→        lhs_token = self.consume()
   311→        if lhs_token.type != TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   312→            raise CircParseError(
   313→                f"Expected identifier for LHS, got '{lhs_token.value}'",
   314→                self.filename, lhs_token.line, lhs_token.col
   315→            )
   316→
   317→        lhs = lhs_token.value
   318→
   319→        # Check = sign
   320→        eq_token = self.consume()
   321→        if eq_token.type != TokenType.EQUALS:
   322→            raise CircParseError(
   323→                f"Expected '=', got '{eq_token.value}'",
   324→                self.filename, eq_token.line, eq_token.col
   325→            )
   326→
   327→        # Parse RHS (expression)
   328→        rhs = self._parse_expression()
   329→
   330→        result['assignments'].append((lhs, rhs))
   331→
   332→        # Consume newline
   333→        if self.pos < len(self.tokens) and self.peek().type == TokenType.NEWLINE:
   334→            self.consume()
   335→
   336→        return lhs, rhs
   337→
   338→    def _parse_expression(self):
   339→        """Parse an expression: identifier, literal, or OP(...)"""
   340→        token = self.consume()
   341→
   342→        if token.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   343→            # Check for forbidden literal X
   344→            if token.value == 'X':
   345→                raise CircParseError(
   346→                    "Forbidden literal 'X' value",
   347→                    self.filename, token.line, token.col
   348→                )
   349→            # Could be a literal (0 or 1) or an identifier
   350→            if token.value in ('0', '1'):
   351→                return ('literal', token.value)
   352→            # Check if it's an operator call
   353→            next_token = self.peek()
   354→            if next_token.type == TokenType.LPAREN:
   355→                return self._parse_operator_call(token)
   356→            return ('identifier', token.value)
   357→        elif token.type == TokenType.LPAREN:
   358→            # Start of operator call
   359→            return self._parse_operator_call_in_parens()
   360→        else:
   361→            raise CircParseError(
   362→                f"Expected expression, got '{token.value}'",
   363→                self.filename, token.line, token.col
   364→            )
   365→
   366→    def _parse_operator_call(self, op_token: Token = None):
   367→        """Parse an operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   368→        if op_token is None:
   369→            op_token = self.consume()
   370→
   371→        op_name = op_token.value.upper()
   372→
   373→        if op_name not in self.OPERATORS:
   374→            raise CircParseError(
   375→                f"Unknown operator '{op_name}'",
   376→                self.filename, op_token.line, op_token.col
   377→            )
   378→
   379→        # Check for opening paren
   380→        paren_token = self.consume()
   381→        if paren_token.type != TokenType.LPAREN:
   382→            raise CircParseError(
   383→                f"Expected '(', got '{paren_token.value}'",
   384→                self.filename, paren_token.line, paren_token.col
   385→            )
   386→
   387→        # Parse arguments
   388→        args = []
   389→        while True:
   390→            # Check for closing paren
   391→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.RPAREN:
   392→                self.consume()
   393→                break
   394→
   395→            arg = self._parse_expression()
   396→            args.append(arg)
   397→
   398→            # Check for comma or closing paren
   399→            next_tok = self.peek()
   400→            if next_tok.type == TokenType.COMMA:
   401→                self.consume()
   402→            elif next_tok.type != TokenType.RPAREN:
   403→                raise CircParseError(
   404→                    f"Expected ',' or ')', got '{next_tok.value}'",
   405→                    self.filename, next_tok.line, next_tok.col
   406→                )
   407→
   408→        # Check arity
   409→        expected = self.OPERATORS[op_name]
   410→        if expected == 1 and len(args) != 1:
   411→            raise ArityError(
   412→                f"Operator '{op_name}' requires exactly 1 argument, got {len(args)}"
   413→            )
   414→        if expected == -1 and len(args) < 2:
   415→            raise ArityError(
   416→                f"Operator '{op_name}' requires at least 2 arguments, got {len(args)}"
   417→            )
   418→
   419→        return ('call', op_name, args)
   420→
   421→    def _parse_operator_call_in_parens(self):
   422→        """Parse an operator call that starts with (OP(...))"""
   423→        # Consume the opening paren
   424→        self.consume()
   425→
   426→        # The next token should be the operator name
   427→        op_token = self.consume()
   428→        if op_token.type != TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   429→            raise CircParseError(
   430→                f"Expected operator name, got '{op_token.value}'",
   431→                self.filename, op_token.line, op_token.col
   432→            )
   433→
   434→        # Parse the operator call
   435→        result = self._parse_operator_call(op_token)
   436→
   437→        # Consume the closing paren for the whole expression
   438→        if self.pos < len(self.tokens) and self.peek().type == TokenType.RPAREN:
   439→            self.consume()
   440→
   441→        return result
   442→
   443→    def peek(self, offset: int = 0) -> Token:
   444→        return self.tokens[self.pos + offset]
   445→
   446→    def consume(self) -> Token:
   447→        token = self.tokens[self.pos]
   448→        self.pos += 1
   449→        return token
   450→
   451→
   452→def parse_file(filepath: str) -> dict:
   453→    """Parse a .circ file and return the parsed result."""
   454→    try:
   455→        with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   456→            text = f.read()
   457→    except FileNotFoundError:
   458→        raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}")
   459→
   460→    tokenizer = Tokenizer(text, filepath)
   461→    tokens = tokenizer.tokenize()
   462→
   463→    parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   464→    return parser.parse()

   466→
   467→# =============================================================================
   468→# Validator
   469→# =============================================================================
   470→
   471→@dataclass
   472→class Circuit:
   473→    inputs: set[str]
   474→    outputs: set[str]
   475→    wires: set[str]
   476→    assignments: list[tuple[str, tuple]]  # (lhs, rhs)
   477→
   478→
   479→def validate_circuit(circuit: dict, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   480→    """Validate a parsed circuit and check for errors."""
   481→    inputs = circuit['inputs']
   482→    outputs = circuit['outputs']
   483→    wires = circuit['wires']
   484→    assignments = circuit['assignments']
   485→
   486→    # Check that all LHS are valid
   487→    for lhs, _ in assignments:
   488→        if lhs in inputs:
   489→            raise InputAssignmentError(f"Cannot assign to input '{lhs}'")
   490→        if lhs not in outputs and lhs not in wires:
   491→            raise UndefinedNameError(f"Undefined signal '{lhs}'")
   492→
   493→    # Build dependency graph for cycle detection
   494→    deps = {name: set() for name in wires | outputs}
   495→
   496→    def collect_deps(expr, signal_name):
   497→        if expr[0] == 'identifier':
   498→            dep_name = expr[1]
   499→            if dep_name in inputs | wires | outputs:
   500→                deps[signal_name].add(dep_name)
   501→        elif expr[0] == 'literal':
   502→            pass
   503→        elif expr[0] == 'call':
   504→            for arg in expr[2]:
   505→                collect_deps(arg, signal_name)
   506→
   507→    for lhs, rhs in assignments:
   508→        collect_deps(rhs, lhs)
   509→
   510→    # Check for cycles
   511→    visited = set()
   512→    rec_stack = set()
   513→    cycle_path = []
   514→
   515→    def dfs(node):
   516→        visited.add(node)
   517→        rec_stack.add(node)
   518→        cycle_path.append(node)
   519→
   520→        for neighbor in deps.get(node, set()):
   521→            if neighbor not in inputs and neighbor not in wires | outputs:
   522→                continue  # Skip non-signal dependencies
   523→            if neighbor not in visited:
   524→                if dfs(neighbor):
   525→                    return True
   526→            elif neighbor in rec_stack:
   527→                # Found a cycle - extract it
   528→                start_idx = cycle_path.index(neighbor)
   529→                cycle = cycle_path[start_idx:] + [neighbor]
   530→                raise CycleError(cycle)
   531→
   532→        cycle_path.pop()
   533→        rec_stack.remove(node)
   534→        return False
   535→
   536→    for node in wires | outputs:
   537→        if node not in visited:
   538→            dfs(node)
   539→
   540→    return Circuit(inputs=inputs, outputs=outputs, wires=wires, assignments=assignments)

   544→# CLI
   545→# =============================================================================
   546→
   547→def output_json(command: str, data: dict):
   548→    """Output JSON to stdout."""
   549→    print(json.dumps(data))
   550→
   551→
   552→def output_plain(command: str, data: dict):
   553→    """Output plain text to stdout."""
   554→    if command == '__version__':
   555→        print(data['version'])
   556→    elif command == 'check':
   557→        # Format inputs and outputs
   558→        inputs = sorted(data['inputs'], key=lambda x: x['name'])
   559→        outputs = sorted(data['outputs'], key=lambda x: x['name'])
   560→        print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(i['name'] for i in inputs)}")
   561→        print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(o['name'] for o in outputs)}")
   562→
   563→
   564→def handle_version(json_flag: bool):
   565→    """Handle --version flag."""
   566→    data = {
   567→        "ok": True,
   568→        "command": "__version__",
   569→        "version": __version__
   570→    }
   571→    if json_flag:
   572→        output_json("__version__", data)
   573→    else:
   574→        output_plain("__version__", data)
   575→    return 0
   576→
   577→
   578→def handle_check(filepath: str, json_flag: bool, filename: str) -> int:
   579→    """Handle the check command."""
   580→    try:
   581→        parsed = parse_file(filepath)
   582→        circuit = validate_circuit(parsed, filepath)
   583→    except FileNotFoundError as e:
   584→        if json_flag:
   585→            print(json.dumps({
   586→                "ok": False,
   587→                "command": "check",
   588→                "exit_code": 1,
   589→                "error": {
   590→                    "type": "FileNotFoundError",
   591→                    "message": str(e),
   592→                    "file": filepath,
   593→                    "line": None,
   594→                    "col": None
   595→                }
   596→            }))
   597→        else:
   598→            print(f"Error: {e}")
   599→        return 1
   600→    except CircoptError as e:
   601→        if json_flag:
   602→            print(json.dumps(e.to_json("check", filepath)))
   603→        else:
   604→            print(f"Error: {e}")
   605→        return e.exit_code
   606→
   607→    # Success
   608→    inputs = sorted([{"name": name, "msb": 0, "lsb": 0} for name in circuit.inputs],
   609→                    key=lambda x: x['name'])
   610→    outputs = sorted([{"name": name, "msb": 0, "lsb": 0} for name in circuit.outputs],
   611→                     key=lambda x: x['name'])
   612→
   613→    data = {
   614→        "ok": True,
   615→        "command": "check",
   616→        "format": "circ",
   617→        "inputs": inputs,
   618→        "outputs": outputs
   619→    }
   620→
   621→    if json_flag:
   622→        output_json("check", data)
   623→    else:
   624→        output_plain("check", data)
   625→
   626→    return 0

   629→def main():
   630→    """Main entry point for the circopt CLI."""
   631→    import sys
   632→
   633→    # If no arguments provided, show error
   634→    if len(sys.argv) == 1:
   635→        # Check for --json flag
   636→        json_flag = '--json' in sys.argv
   637→        if json_flag:
   638→            print(json.dumps({

   647→                    "col": None

   650→        else:
   651→            print("Error: No command specified")
   652→            print("Usage: python circopt.py <command> [options]")
   653→            print("Commands: check")
   654→        return 1
   655→
   656→    # Check for global flags
   657→    args = sys.argv[1:]
   658→
   659→    # Handle --help
   660→    if '--help' in args:
   661→        parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   662→            prog="circopt",
   663→            description="Circuit optimizer and validator for .circ files"
   664→        )
   665→        parser.add_argument('--help', action='store_true', help='Show help message')
   666→        parser.add_argument('--version', action='store_true', help='Show version')
   667→        parser.add_argument('--json', action='store_true', help='Output in JSON format')
   668→        parser.print_help()
   669→        return 0
   670→
   671→    # Handle --version
   672→    if '--version' in args:
   673→        json_flag = '--json' in args
   674→        return handle_version(json_flag)
   675→
   676→    # Parse the command
   677→    if args[0] == 'check':
   678→        json_flag = '--json' in args
   679→        # Find the filename (first non-flag argument after 'check')
   680→        filename = None
   681→        for arg in args[1:]:
   682→            if arg != '--json':
   683→                filename = arg
   684→                break
   685→
   686→        if filename is None:
   687→            if json_flag:
   688→                print(json.dumps({
   689→                    "ok": False,
   690→                    "command": "check",
   691→                    "exit_code": 1,
   692→                    "error": {
   693→                        "type": "CliUsageError",
   694→                        "message": "Missing required argument: <file.circ>",
   695→                        "file": None,
   696→                        "line": None,
   697→                        "col": None
   698→                    }
   699→                }))
   700→            else:
   701→                print("Error: Missing required argument: <file.circ>")
   702→            return 1
   703→
   704→        return handle_check(filename, json_flag, "check")
   705→    else:
   706→        json_flag = '--json' in args
   707→        if json_flag:

   709→                "ok": False,
   710→                "command": "__cli__",

   560→def evaluate_circuit(
   561→    circuit: Circuit,

   565→) -> dict[str, int]:

   571→        default: Default value for inputs not provided (0 or 1).

   573→
   574→    Returns:

   576→
   577→    Raises:
   578→        MissingInputError: If a required input is missing.
   579→        UnknownInputError: If an unknown input is provided and allow_extra is False.
   580→        InputValueParseError: If an input value is not 0 or 1.
   581→    """
   582→    # Validate input values
   583→    for name, value in inputs.items():
   584→        if value not in (0, 1):
   585→            raise InputValueParseError(

   618→        'NAND': lambda args: 1 - int(all(args)),
   619→        'NOR': lambda args: 1 - int(any(args)),

class Tokenizer:
    """Tokenizer for .circ files."""

    def __init__(self, text: str, filename: str):
        self.text = text
        self.filename = filename
        self.pos = 0
        self.line = 1
        self.col = 1
        self.tokens: list[Token] = []

    def tokenize(self) -> list[Token]:
        while self.pos < len(self.text):
            ch = self.text[self.pos]
            start_pos = self.pos

            if ch.isspace():
                if ch == '\n':
                    self._add_token(TokenType.NEWLINE, '\n', self.line, self.col)
                    self.line += 1
                    self.col = 1
                    self.pos += 1
                else:
                    self.col += 1
                    self.pos += 1
            elif ch == '#':
                # Skip comment until end of line
                while self.pos < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos] != '\n':
                    self.pos += 1
                    self.col += 1
            elif ch.isalpha() or ch == '_':
                start_line = self.line
                start_col = self.col
                while self.pos < len(self.text) and (self.text[self.pos].isalnum() or self.text[self.pos] == '_'):
                    self.pos += 1
                    self.col += 1
                value = self.text[start_col - 1:self.pos]
                self._add_token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, value, start_line, start_col)
            elif ch.isdigit():
                start_line = self.line
                start_col = self.col
                while self.pos < len(self.text) and self.text[self.pos].isdigit():
                    self.pos += 1
                    self.col += 1
                value = self.text[start_col - 1:self.pos]
                self._add_token(TokenType.NUMBER, value, start_line, start_col)
            elif ch == '(':
                self._add_token(TokenType.LPAREN, '(', self.line, self.col)
                self.pos += 1
                self.col += 1
            elif ch == ')':
                self._add_token(TokenType.RPAREN, ')', self.line, self.col)
                self.pos += 1
                self.col += 1
            elif ch == ',':
                self._add_token(TokenType.COMMA, ',', self.line, self.col)
                self.pos += 1
                self.col += 1
            elif ch == '=':
                self._add_token(TokenType.EQUALS, '=', self.line, self.col)
                self.pos += 1
                self.col += 1
            else:
                raise CircParseError(
                    f"Unexpected character '{ch}'",
                    self.filename, self.line, self.col
                )

        self._add_token(TokenType.EOF, '', self.line, self.col)
        return self.tokens

    def _add_token(self, token_type: TokenType, value: str, line: int = None, col: int = None):
        if line is None:
            line = self.line
        if col is None:
            col = self.col
        self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, line, col))


class Parser:
    """Parser for .circ files."""

    # Supported operators and their arities
    OPERATORS = {
        'NOT': 1,
        'BUF': 1,
        'AND': -1,  # >= 2
        'OR': -1,
        'XOR': -1,
        'NAND': -1,
        'NOR': -1,
        'XNOR': -1,
    }

    def __init__(self, tokens: list[Token], filename: str):
        self.tokens = tokens
        self.filename = filename
        self.pos = 0
        self.assignments_started = False
